FEB 14 '91 14:54 TOWN OF LOS GATOS 408 3548431                                P.2


                                    [IMAGE: Two cat figures]
                                    TOWN of LOS GATOS
                                    OFFICE OF THE TOWN CLERK
                                    (408) 354-6834

February 14, 1991

LEGAL DEPARTMENT
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807


RE: REQUEST FOR LEGAL OPINION


Dear Sirs:

The Town of Los Gatos has recently formed a Redevelopment Agency which is not a
separate entity from the Town Council. The duties of the Agency are an addition to the
Town Council's normal duties. The Clerk's Office now questions whether our Elected
Officials, Planning Commissioners, Town Manager, Town Treasurer and Town Attorney
should complete an additional Form 721. An alternative to the completion of a separate
Form 721 would be to add Redevelopment Agency to the City Office line.

The Town of Los Gatos, in its "conflict of interest" code, does require certain Department
Managers to complete and file a Form 730 with the Town Clerk's Office. Once again we
question if it is necessary to file two forms, one for the Town and one for the
Redevelopment Agency, or if one form listing both entities is sufficient, or, indeed, if the
Redevelopment Agency needs to be included at all.

A third question has arisen regarding acting and interim officials and employees and the
necessity of their filing either the 721 or 730. At the moment we have an acting Town
Attorney and in the recent past we had an Interim Town Manager. At present we have an
Interim Town Engineer. If our internal "conflict of interest" code states Department
Managers does it follow that acting or interim Managers should also be filing forms when
assuming and/or leaving their duties?

Also, if a Consultant is hired specifically to assist with matters pertaining to the
Redevelopment Agency or other Town duties, is it necessary for the Consultant to complete
conflict of interest forms?




                                            1

CIVIC CENTER • 110 EAST MAIN STREET • P.O. BOX 949 • LOS GATOS, CALIFORNIA 95031

FEB 14 '91 15:20                           408 3548431    PAGE 002

FEB 14 '91 14:54 TOWN OF LOS GATOS 408 3548431 P.3

Enclosed is Town Ordinance 1576, adopted April 18, 1983 which refers to Consultants.
Also enclosed is Town Ordinance 1705, adopted January 6, 1987 which, I believe,
supersedes Ordinance 1576. Also included is letter from David Beatty, Attorney for
Redevelopment referring to conflict of interest and which was included in the minutes of
the Town Council and Redevelopment Agency, January 22, 1990.

Further, enclosed is a Memorandum from our Acting Counsel indicating that a "/" (slash)
is acceptable. However, our telephone conversation with FPPC this morning indicated
that this is not necessary.

Do we need to reinstate an Ordinance to cover consultants and, if so, how do you suggest
it be worded? Is the code sufficient to cope with not having to fill out disclosure forms
and, if we are not covered at this time, do the consultants fill out Form 721 or Form 730?

This communication is being sent by certified mail #P174 278 915: Return Receipt
Requested. We request a written response from your agency.

This communication is also being FAXED on February 14, 1991 and we would GREATLY
APPRECIATE A RESPONSE BY FAX on February 15, 1991 AND NO LATER THAN
FEBRUARY 19, 1991. The Town Council is meeting to ACT ON A REDEVELOPMENT
MATTER ON FEBRUARY 19, 1991 and we wish to be in compliance with the Law before
taking action.

We have also enclosed copies of our Redevelopment Resolution 1990-R7 which refers to
the Town's Conflict of Interest Code.

Please address all return communications to MARIAN V. COSGROVE, CLERK OF THE
TOWN OF LOS GATOS and response by FAX should include a PRINTED NAME as well
as a SIGNATURE.

If you need additional information, or clarification of our request, please do not hesitate
to contact me at (408)354-6834.

Sincerely

Marian V. Cosgrove
Clerk of the Town of Los Gatos

FEB 14 '91 15:01 408 3548431 PAGE.003

FEB 14 '91 14:55 TOWN OF LOS GATOS 408 3548431                P.4


                                MEMORANDUM


TO:        David W. Knapp, Agency Executive Director

FROM:      Natalie E. West, Agency Counsel [handwritten: NOTED]

DATE:      February 14, 1991

RE:        Statements of Economic Interest

Per my telephone conversation with Lynn Martin, staff consultant at State Fair Political
Practices Commission, Los Gatos redevelopment agency members are not required to file
any statements of economic interest other than what they already file.

Because Redevelopment Agency consists solely of Town Councilmembers, the
Councilmembers should just file a 721 in April as they usually do. The agency is not
included in the Town's conflict of interest code.

When the annual statement is filed, each Councilmember should simply indicate on cover
sheet that it is a statement filed by a "Town Council Member/Redevelopment Agency
Member".

I hope this information is of assistance.

cc:   Marian V. Cosgrove, Town Clerk
      Lee Bowman, Redevelopment Planning Director




FEB 14 '91 15:01                    408 3548431    PAGE.004

FEB 14 '91 14:56 TOWN OF LOS GATOS 408 3548431                                P.5


                    To:  Members of the Town Council
                         of the Town of Los Gatos
                         and
                         Members of the Redevelopment Agency
                         of the Town of Los Gatos


                    The purpose of this letter is to inform all concerned of the requirements of
        Section 33130 of the Health and Safety Code (including changes which took effect
        January 1, 1986) which (1) prohibits the acquisition of a financial interest in a
        redevelopment project area; and (2) requires the disclosure of any existing financial
        interest within the project area. This Section is separate from and imposes different
        interest within the project area. This Section is separate from and imposes different
        requirements from those of the Political Reform Act of 1974.

                    The requirements of the Section apply to any agency or county officer or
        employee who, in the course of his or her duties, is required to help formulate or to
        approve plans or policies for redevelopment of a project area. Thus, it includes not
        only the Redevelopment Agency and Town Council members, but also the Town
        Manager, Planning Commissioners, and Town and Agency staff.

                    This Section has three specific provisions. First, it requires that any such
        officer or employee who owns or has any direct or indirect financial interest in any
        property included within a project area immediately make a written disclosure of
        that interest to the Agency and the Town Council. Failure to make such disclosure
        constitutes misconduct in office. Preferably, this disclosure should be done when a
        survey area is designated and should be done in all cases immediately after the
        Planning Commission has selected a project area.

                    Second, except as described in the following paragraphs, Section 33130 strictly
        prohibits any officer or employee from acquiring an interest in any property
        included within a project area. The Section provides an exception for an officer or
        employee who is acquiring an interest as an owner or is reentering a business and
        that person has owned a substantially equal interest as that being acquired for the
        three years immediately preceding the selection of the project area.

                    Third, Section 33130 provides that a rental agreement or lease of property
        for which meets all of the conditions described below is not an interest in property
        purposes of the prohibition. The four conditions are:

                    1.      The rental or lease agreement contains terms which are
                    substantially equivalent to the terms of a lease available to the public for comparable
                    property in the project area.


                                                                                          1/9/90    (FOR INE

        LGA/DsdLlr/CDC


FEB 14 '91 15:02                                           408 3548431      PAGE.005

FEB 14 '91 14:56 TOWN OF LOS GATOS 408 3548431 P.6

2. The rental or lease agreement prohibits subleasing the property
for a rate higher than the one set in the original agreement.

3. The property being rented is used in the pursuit of the principal
business, occupation or profession of the officer or employee.

4. The officer or employee must immediately make a written
disclosure of the lease or rental agreement to the Agency and the legislative body

In addition, Section 33130.5 permits an officer, employee, consultant or agent
of the Agency or Town to purchase or lease property within a project area for
personal residential use, subject, however, to certain restrictions. The Agency must
first certify that the improvements to be built or other work to be done on the
property to be leased or purchased have been completed or that no work needs to be
done on the property. Any such officer or employee who purchases or leases such
property must immediately disclose that fact in writing to the Agency and the Town
Council and the disclosure will be entered on the Agency's minutes. Any such
officer or employee is thereafter disqualified from voting on any matters directly
affecting such lease, purchase or residency. A failure to disclose such interest
constitutes misconduct in office.

Disclosure forms will be available from the Agency. Completed forms also
will be kept on file in the Agency offices.

This letter shall be entered into the minutes of the Town Council of the
Town of Los Gatos and the Redevelopment Agency of the Town of Los Gatos

Very truly yours,

McDONOUGH, HOLLAND & ALLEN
A Professional Corporation


By ________________________
David F. Beatty

FEB 14 '91 14:57 TOWN OF LOS GATOS 408 3548431

ORDINANCE NO. 1576

ORDINANCE AMENDING SECTION 2-61 OF THE TOWN CODE TO ADD
MEMBERS OF THE PLANNING COMMISSION AND CONSULTANTS AS
EMPLOYEES COVERED BY CONFLICT OF INTEREST PROVISIONS


The Town Council of Los Gatos hereby ORDAINS:

SECTION 1.

Section 2-61 of the Town Code is amended to read as follows:

"Sec. 2-61. Designated employees.

This section shall constitute the Appendix referred to in Section 2 of the

standardized code. The Council hereby finds that the following persons make or par-

ticipate in the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on

financial interests, and are therefore designated employees: all elected Town

officials, members of the Planning Commission, the Town Attorney, the Town Manager,

the Assistant Town Manager, all Department Heads appointed by the Town Manager, and

any consultants hired by the Council; provided, that with respect to consultants the

Town Manager may determine in writing that a particular consultant, although a

"designated employee," is hired to perform a range of duties that are limited in

scope and thus is not required to comply with the disclosure requirements described

in this section. Such determination shall include a description of the consultant's

duties and, based upon that description, a statement of the extent of disclosure

requirements. A copy of any such determination shall be forwarded to the Town

Council. Nothing herein excuses any consultant from any other provision of this

Conflict of Interest Code."

SECTION 2.

This ordinance takes effect thirty (30) days after the date it is adopted.

Within fifteen (15) days after this ordinance is adopted, the Town Clerk shall cause

it to be published once in a newspaper of general circulation published and circulated

in the Town.


408 3548431 PAGE.007

This ordinance was introduced at a regular meeting of the Town Council of

the Town of Los Gatos on April 13, 1983 , and adopted by the following vote

as an ordinance of the Town of Los Gatos at a regular meeting of the Town Council

on May 2, 1983 .


AYES: COUNCILMEMBERS Joanne Benjamin, Terrence J. Daily, Thomas
J. Ferriso, Marlyn J. Rasmussen and Mayor Brent N. Ventura

NOES: COUNCILMEMBERS None

ABSTAIN: COUNCILMEMBERS None

ABSENT: COUNCILMEMBERS None


SIGNED: [signature]
Mayor of the Town of Los Gatos


ATTEST:

[signature]
Clerk of the Town of Los Gatos

FEB 14 '91 14:58 TOWN OF LOS GATOS 40S 3548431                                P.9


                              RESOLUTION 1990 - 7RD


              RESOLUTION OF THE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY OF
              THE TOWN OF LOS GATOS ADOPTING A CONFLICT OF
              INTEREST CODE APPLICABLE TO THE MEMBER
              OF THE AGENCY PURSUANT TO THE POLITICAL
                        REFORM ACT OF 1974




         THE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY OF THE TOWN OF LOS GATOS DOES HEREBY

RESOLVE AS FOLLOWS:



         Section 1.        Adoption of Conflict of Interest Code. In compliance with
Section 87300 of the Government Code, the Redevelopment Agency of the Town of Los Gatos
(the "Agency") hereby adopts the "Conflict of Interest Code of the Officers and Employees of the
Redevelopment Agency of the Town of Los Gatos," in the form attached to this resolution and
incorporated herein by reference.

         Section 2.        Application of Code. This conflict of Interest Code shall be
applicable to members of the Agency.

         Section 3.        Disclosure. Agency members, in their capacity as Town Council
of the Town of Los Gatos, are already required to disclose investments, interest in real property
and income under Section 87200 et seq. of the Government Code within the jurisdiction of the
Agency. Therefore, no other or additional disclosure requirements are imposed by this Conflict of
Interest Code.

         Section 4.        Circumstances Requiring Disqualification. No Agency member
shall, make, participate in making or use his or her official positions to influence the making of
any governmental decision which will foreseeably have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on a financial interest as defined in Section 87103 of the
Government Code, unless his or her participation is legally required for the decision to be made.

Section 5.

Send to Code-Reviewing Body. The Agency Secretary is hereby
authorized and directed to forward a certified copy of this resolution to the Town Council for
review and filing.

22nd day of January, 1990, by the following vote:

FEB 14 '91 15:05                408 3548431  PAGE.011

FEB 14 '91 14:59 TOWN OF LOS GATOS 408 3548431                P.12




                        CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE
                                OF THE
                            OFFICERS AND EMPLOYEES
                                  OF THE
                    REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY OF THE TOWN OF LOS GATOS


     Section 100. Purpose. The Political Reform Act, Government Code Section
81000 et seq., requires every state or local government agency to adopt and
promulgate a Conflict of Interest Code.

     Section 200. Designated Positions. The positions listed on Exhibit A, attached
hereto, are designated positions. Officers and employees holding these positions are
designated employees and are deemed to make, or participate in the making of,
decisions which may foreseeably have a material financial effect on a financial
interest of the designated employee.

     Designated employees, in their capacities as
Section 300. Existing Code.
officials and employees of the Town of Los Gatos, are already designated employees
pursuant to a Conflict of Interest Code of the Town of Los Gatos adopted under the
Political Reform Act of 1974 for the jurisdiction of the Agency, and pursuant to that
Code are required to file disclosure statements and are prohibited from making or
participating in the making of any governmental decision which will foreseeably
have a material financial effect upon a financial interest of the designated employee.

     Section 400. Adoption by Reference. The terms and provisions of the
Conflict of Interest Code of the Town of Los Gatos and any amendments to it duly
adopted, along with Exhibit A attached hereto, are hereby incorporated by reference
and constitute the Conflict of Interest Code of the Officers and Employees of the
Redevelopment Agency of the Town of Los Gatos.

     Section 500. Compliance. Compliance with all of the terms and conditions of
the Conflict of Interest Code of the Town of Los Gatos shall be deemed to be
compliance with this Conflict of Interest Code.




                                                                           1/31/90

                            ORDINANCE NO: 1705

        ORDINANCE AMENDING THE TOWN CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE
        TO INCLUDE THE FINANCE ADMINISTRATOR AS A DESIGNATED EMPLOYEE

                              SECTION 1.

            Section 2-61 of the Town Code is hereby amended to read as
        follows:

            Sec. 2-61. Designated Employees.

                This section shall constitute the Appendix referred to in
            Section 2 of the standardized code. The Council hereby
            finds that the following persons make or participate in the
            making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material
            effect on financial interests, and are therefor designated
            as all elected Town officials, the Town Attorney,
            employees:
            the Town Manager, the Assistant Town Manager, the Finance
            the Town Administrator and all Department Heads appointed by the Town
            Manager.

                              SECTION 2.

            This ordinance takes effect thirty (30) days after the date it is
        adopted. Within fifteen (15) days after this ordinance is adopted, the
        Town Clerk shall cause it to be published once in a newspaper of general
        circulation published and circulated in the Town.

Los Gatos Town Code

§ 2-63

Town Officials. The Town Manager, the Town Attorney, the Finance Administrator and all appointed Heads appointed by the Town Manager shall constitute the Town Manager's Cabinet. (Ord. No. 1576, § 1 part: Ord. No. 1705, § 1)

§ 2-62. Disclosure categories.

See § 2-62. Disclosure categories.
Upon the adoption of the prescribed code, all designated employees shall constitute the prescribed code. All designated employees' financial interests that are required to be reported in Government Code Section 87201. (Ord. No. 1576, § 1 part.)

Sec. 2-63. Definition of indirect interest.

See § 2-63. Definition of indirect interest.
In Government Code Section 87113 defines "financial interest" within the meaning of Section 87101 and in accordance with the first 'indirect interest' as any interest in a matter of investment officers listed in Section 87103, any investment interest owned by the immediate family of a public official, and any interest owned by a dependent or relative. (See § 1 part.)
